# Moonshine GNOT examples

Small, runnable [GNOT](../../../gnot) flows that print poems. They double as the
bundled `samples` you can run inside moonshine and as copy-paste starting points.

## Run them

Inside the moonshine shell (or via `moonshine -c`):

```
samples                       # list the bundled poems
samples grasshopper           # run one
```

The `samples` command runs an embedded copy, so it works from any directory. You
can also run a file directly:

```
moonshine examples/grasshopper.gnot
```

## What's here

| File | Poem |
| :--- | :--- |
| `grasshopper.gnot` | e.e. cummings — letters leap and rearrange into "grasshopper" |
| `falling-leaf.gnot` | e.e. cummings "l(a" — a leaf falls; loneliness |
| `since-feeling-is-first.gnot` | e.e. cummings — feeling over the syntax of things |

## GNOT shape (poetry profile)

A file needs a header; an inline `gnot ...` string does not (the leading `gnot`
token is consumed by the shell):

```
gnot grasshopper v0.9        # header: name + version  (files only)
profile poetry               # profile                 (files only)
is main                      # flow declaration
  self, echo("who, as we look")   # clause: subject, predicate(args)
```

Each clause is `target <- subject, predicate` (binding) or `subject, predicate`
(terminal). `echo(...)` runs as a command, so the lines print in order. Bindings
flow into later clauses, e.g. `user <- self, whoami` then `self, echo("hi ", user)`.
